content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
May 6,
1992
Stephen G.
Lodge
National Confectioners Association
7900 Westpark Dr.,
Suite A-320
McLean, Virginia
22102—4203
Re:
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No.
1—92—212
Dear Mr.
Lodge:
You have requested advice regarding the campaign provisions
of the Political Reform Act
(the Act”).
1
/
Because your question
concerns past conduct,
we treat your letter as a request for
informal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c) (4) •2/
QUESTION
Was the National Confectioners Association
(“NCA”)
required
to file a major donor report if the ballot measure committee to
which NCA made a $25,000 contribution during 1991 disbanded the
ballot measure qualification process before the major donor
statement was due?
CONCLUSION
Yes,
a major donor campaign statement
(Form 461)
was required
to be filed no later than January 31,
1992,
covering the period
January 1,
1991 through December 31,
1991.
1/
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et .
All references to regulations are to Title
2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2/
Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114;
2 Cal.
Code of Regs.
Section
18329 Cc) (3).)
42RJ Street, Suite 800
• P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 9804-0807 •
(Q16 322-660


FACTS
The
NCA made a contribution of $25,000 in November 1991 to
the Sales Tax Revolt Committee
(I.D.
# 911793),
organizers of the
“Necessities of Life” ballot initiative campaign.
In January
1992,
NCA was notified that the ballot initiative campaign was
being abandoned.
In a telephone conversation with me on April 23,
1992,
you stated that this was NCA’s first contribution made to a
California candidate, ballot measure, or committee.
You also
stated that NCA made a political contribution of over $1,000 to
another state ballot measure committee in 1992.
ANALYSIS
The Act requires California state and local committees to
file periodic disclosure reports.
(Sections 84200—84204.)
An
organization will qualify as a committee if it:
(a)
Receives contributions totaling one
thousand
($1,000)
or more in a calendar year.
(b)
Makes independent expenditures totaling
one thousand dollars
($1,000)
or more in a calendar
year; or
(c)
Makes contributions totaling ten thousand
dollars
($10,000)
or more in a calendar year to or
at the behest of candidates or committees.
Section 82013.
As I explained to you in our telephone conversation of April
23,
1992,
because NCA is an organization which receives funds from
its members, but is formed primarily for purposes other than
making contributions to California candidates, ballot measures,
or
committees,
NCA became a major donor committee the first year it
made contributions totaling $10,000 or more.
(Section 82013(c)).
3
!
Therefore,
NCA qualified as a committee when it made the $25,000
contribution to the Sales Tax Revolt Committee and was required to
file a major donor campaign statement
(Form 461)
covering the
period January
1 through December 31,
1991.
The statement was due
January 31,
1992.
The fact that the Sales Tax Revolt Committee
disbanded its ballot measure campaign in 1992 does not affect
-
NCA’s filing requirements.
3d”
Enclosed is
a fact sheet entitled Organizations Which Receive
Funds for Purposes Other Than Making Contributions to or
Expenditures on Behalf of California State and Local Candidates,
Ballot Measures or Committees.


Because NCA established a “history” of making contributions
in connection with California elections it will qualify as a
recipient committee in any future calendar year in which it makes
contributions or expenditures totaling $1,000 or more to
California candidates or committees.
(Section 82013(a)).
You
stated that NCA made a contribution in excess of $1,000 in 1992 to
a ballot measure committee.
On April 24,
1992,
our office mailed
NCA a Statement of Organization
(Form 410),
Campaign Disclosure
Statement
(Form 420)
and the Campaign Disclosure Information
Manual C.
The Statement of Organization
(Form 410)
is required to
be filed within 10 days of NCA qualifying as a recipient
committee.
In addition, if NCA’s 1992 contribution was made
between January
1 and March 17,
1992,
a campaign statement
(Form
420)
was due on March 22,
1992.
If the contribution was made
after March 17th,
NCA’s first campaign statement will be due on
May 21,
1992,
covering the period January 1,
1992 through May 16,
1992.
Please do not hesitate to contact me at
(916)
322-5662 if you
have additional questions.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
eneral
sel
Lynda Cassady
Political Reform Consultant
Enclosure
